problem.title: Delete Node in a Linked List
problem.description: Write a function to delete a node in a singly-linked list.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Value Copy
solutions.description: Since we only have a reference to the node to delete (not its predecessor), copy the next node's value into the current node and then skip the next node. This effectively removes the target node's data from the list in O(1) time without needing to traverse from the head.
solutions.code_python:
# class ListNode: def __init__(self, val=0): self.val=val; self.next=None
def deleteNode(node: object) -> None:
    node.val = node.next.val
    node.next = node.next.next
